samples.raw_output:
```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.7,
    "corrections": [
        "i had a simular problem" -> "I had a similar problem",
        "Here is the tips" -> "Here are the tips",
        "find this apps" -> "find these apps",
        "spend a lot of time to TV" -> "spend a lot of time on TV",
        "leave from useless groups" -> "leave useless groups",
        "enought energy" -> "enough energy",
        "write me at personal messages" -> "send me a private message"
    ],
    "feedback": "The student is able to communicate a clear message and provide structured advice. While there are several basic grammar and spelling errors (e.g., 'simular', 'this apps', 'leave from'), the vocabulary is appropriate for the task and the use of a numbered list shows an ability to organize thoughts logically. The text is more advanced than A2 because it attempts to give a series of recommendations and uses modal verbs ('should', 'can') effectively to provide advice."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B2",
    "grammar_score": 0.8,
    "corrections": [
        "decition -> decision",
        "tips how to get it -> tips on how to get it",
        "options how to organize -> options for organizing / ways to organize",
        "each my day -> each of my days / my days",
        "during a day -> during the day",
        "other's works -> others' work / the work of others",
        "But very important thing -> But a very important thing"
    ],
    "feedback": "The student demonstrates a strong B2 level. They use complex sentence structures (e.g., 'Also, though you shouldn't use other's works a lot, it's actually ok to...') and a good range of vocabulary ('overload yourself', 'abuse this help', 'long periods of free time'). The flow is natural, and the ideas are well-organized with appropriate discourse markers. While there are some minor spelling errors ('decition') and small grammatical slips regarding articles and prepositions, these do not impede communication and are typical for an independent user of the language."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.5,
    "corrections": [
        "I want to tell you about two of my own tips",
        "which help me stay happy with a very hard lifestyle",
        "Firstly, I recommend communicating more with other people.",
        "They can help you look at life more simply.",
        "In the future, good networking will give you a lot of opportunities.",
        "Secondly, you need to track your time; practice time management.",
        "If you think that you study all the time and don't have free time, try noting your day.",
        "In my experience, all the guys who do this find some bad habits that take up really a lot of time.",
        "Delete them and live more freely.",
        "I believe my tips will help you.",
        "If not, you are unlucky"
    ],
    "feedback": "The student demonstrates a B1 level of proficiency. While there are frequent grammatical errors (especially with prepositions, verb forms, and spelling), the student is able to express complex ideas and provide structured advice. The use of discourse markers like 'Firstly' and 'Secondly', and the attempt at more advanced vocabulary such as 'networking', 'opportunity', and 'time management', push this above an A2 level. The meaning is clear throughout the text despite the linguistic slips."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.65,
    "corrections": [
        "Hellow -> Hello",
        "and me to -> and me too",
        "In first semester -> In the first semester",
        "wery big problem -> a very big problem",
        "how can I fix it -> how I can fix it",
        "I will give you them -> I will share them with you",
        "enouth -> enough",
        "do sport well -> exercise regularly",
        "bust your productivity -> boost your productivity"
    ],
    "feedback": "The student demonstrates a B1 level of proficiency. They are able to express complex ideas (such as task prioritization and productivity) and organize their thoughts logically using a list format. While there are several spelling errors ('wery', 'enouth') and grammatical slips (word order in indirect questions, missing articles), these do not impede communication. The use of terms like 'task-management system' and 'prioritize' shows a vocabulary level beyond basic A2."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.5,
    "corrections": [
        "Somebody can solve it, some cannot.",
        "It is easy.",
        "Now I want to give some tips to make difficult study easier.",
        "Try to rewrite solutions from other students.",
        "what your friend wrote",
        "It is not an obvious tip, but it really works.",
        "your brain relaxes and does not do anything",
        "spend less time doing homework than before training.",
        "More sleep means more energy at work.",
        "It is obvious.",
        "If you sleep approximately three hours",
        "Do homework after receiving it.",
        "It is obvious too.",
        "you don't spend time in the future",
        "you will have better marks than if you slept in the first iteration.",
        "These were four tips for making your education easier."
    ],
    "feedback": "The student is operating at a B1 level. While there are frequent grammatical errors (subject-verb agreement, word choice, and spelling), the student successfully communicates complex ideas and provides a structured argument with multiple points. The use of a structured list and the ability to explain the 'why' behind their tips (e.g., the connection between sport and brain relaxation) demonstrates an independent user's ability to convey meaning beyond simple sentences. The vocabulary is sufficient to handle the task, and the overall flow is logical."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.8,
    "corrections": [
        "I think that we have it... (Remove the comma after 'think')",
        "split them into smaller ones (Remove 'the' before 'smaller ones')",
        "If you stick to these tips (Change 'this' to 'these' for plural agreement)",
        "you wouldn't think (Correct spelling of 'wouldn't')"
    ],
    "feedback": "The student demonstrates a good ability to communicate a clear message with a logical structure. They use helpful discourse markers ('Firstly', 'Also') and a variety of sentence structures. The vocabulary is appropriate for the task, and the use of a conditional sentence ('If you stick to...') shows an attempt at more complex grammar. While there are a few minor errors in punctuation and plural agreement, they do not hinder understanding. The text is slightly shorter than the requested 10 sentences, but the quality of the language is consistent with a B1 level."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.7,
    "corrections": [
        "Everyone got same time a day -> Everyone has the same amount of time in a day",
        "study good with it -> study well with it",
        "all homeworks -> all homework (homework is uncountable)",
        "Also Its important -> Also, it's important",
        "If subject seems not intresting -> If a subject seems uninteresting",
        "its not needed -> it's not needed"
    ],
    "feedback": "The student demonstrates a B1 level of proficiency. They are able to express complex ideas, such as prioritizing tasks based on their utility for future courses, and use a good range of vocabulary ('allocate', 'deadlines', 'exhausted', 'set priorities'). The text is well-structured with a clear introduction, body, and conclusion. While there are several grammatical errors (e.g., using 'good' instead of 'well', treating 'homework' as a countable noun, and some punctuation/capitalization slips), these do not impede the meaning of the text. The ability to provide nuanced advice and organize the response logically justifies a B1 rating."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.75,
    "corrections": [
        "I cannnot agree with this point. -> I cannot agree with this point of view.",
        "But there is different situation. -> But there is a different situation.",
        "in other difficult area. -> in other difficult areas.",
        "In the university people study -> At university, people study",
        "And knowledge that university gives is complex. -> Furthermore, the knowledge that a university provides is comprehensive."
    ],
    "feedback": "The student demonstrates a clear ability to organize an argument with an introduction, supporting paragraphs, and a conclusion. The vocabulary is appropriate for the task, and the student successfully expresses complex ideas (e.g., the trade-off between immediate work experience and the exploratory nature of university). While there are some grammatical errors—specifically with articles ('a different situation') and noun plurality ('difficult areas')—they do not impede communication. The text is more advanced than A2 due to its structural coherence and the ability to handle an abstract opinion task, but it lacks the linguistic flexibility and sophisticated vocabulary required for B2."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.5,
    "corrections": [
        "our planet has became more polluted place -> our planet has become a more polluted place",
        "Does this pollutions affect environmental problems? -> Does this pollution contribute to environmental problems?",
        "making pollution problem looks not vital but dumb -> making the pollution problem look not vital, but trivial",
        "It cause politicians do not look -> It causes politicians to ignore",
        "People who cares about environmental health significantly overreacting -> People who care about environmental health are significantly overreacting",
        "climate changes is not just fantasies -> climate change is not just the fantasy",
        "only people responsible for pollutions -> only people are responsible for pollution",
        "making Earth more clear -> making the Earth cleaner"
    ],
    "feedback": "The student demonstrates a B1 level of proficiency. They are able to construct a coherent four-paragraph essay and express complex opinions regarding environmentalism and political perception. The vocabulary is appropriate for the task (e.g., 'eco-activists', 'significantly', 'vital'), and the student successfully uses rhetorical questions to engage the reader. However, there are frequent grammatical errors, particularly with subject-verb agreement ('People who cares'), verb tenses ('has became'), and the use of articles. Despite these errors, the meaning remains clear throughout the text."
}
```
